---
id: BUG-018
title: dispatch prepare conflates work-definition and context references, so real tasks cannot launch
depends_on: ["TASK-CORE-DB-001","STORE-003"]
write_set: ["src/gateway/run-spec*","src/tasks/context-pack*","src/tasks/service*","src/schema/run-spec*","src/cli/commands/dispatch*","src/orchestration/agent-executor*","src/serve/**","content/cli.md"]
requirements: ["provider-agnostic","deterministic-context","immutable-work-definition"]
evidence_required: ["red-test-output","reference-type-fixtures","cross-surface-parity","verify-root","final-sha","independent-review"]
---

## Problem

`dispatch prepare --task <packet@version>` advertises task dispatch, but the current
implementation copies the argument into both `taskRef` and `dispatchRef` and resolves
it as a Context Policy item. A real packet such as `ROLE-CONFIG-001@1` is rejected as
`missing context item`. Normal board work therefore cannot enter the registered
adapter path and operators fall back to direct harness prompts.

## Task

Connect dispatch preparation to the authoritative immutable work definition.

1. Replace the ambiguous duplicated string with distinct typed references for work
   definition, workflow effect and context entries. Their schemas and identities must
   not be interchangeable.
2. Resolve the packet/work-definition reference from the task/plan SoT, including an
   incrementing definition version and canonical digest. Reject missing, stale,
   mutable-without-version and status-ineligible definitions with distinct codes.
3. Derive `ContextCompileInput` deterministically from the work definition, active
   workflow effect, role and phase. Callers do not repeat task tags, policy refs,
   requirements or dependencies as ad hoc CLI flags.
4. Bind the resulting RunSpec to the exact work-definition version/digest, workflow
   effect and ContextPack receipt. Repreparing identical inputs is idempotent; a changed
   definition produces a new version and cannot mutate an existing RunSpec.
5. Expose the same preparation capability to CLI, workflow coordinator and serve.
   Direct adapter calls cannot construct or substitute these refs.

## RED test

- Preparing a real eligible packet succeeds without a matching Context Policy id.
- A Context Policy id passed as a work-definition ref is rejected by type.
- Missing, stale and unversioned work definitions return distinct stable codes.
- Changing a packet definition increments its version and invalidates the old
  preparation without timestamp/PID identity.
- CLI, coordinator and serve preparation yield the same semantic RunSpec digest.

## Acceptance

- Actual board work can reach registered adapters through one supported preparation
  capability.
- Work identity and context identity remain separate and versioned.
- Context selection is derived, minimal and receipted; no manual tag/ref assembly.
- Full verification and independent architecture review pass.

## Stop conditions

- No special case for `ROLE-CONFIG-001`, bundled roles, OpenCode or this repository.
- No fallback that treats arbitrary strings as multiple reference kinds.
- No caller-authored digest/version and no transcript/prompt as work authority.

## Evidence

Provide real-packet fixture, reference-type rejection fixtures, version/digest and
idempotency receipts, cross-surface parity, full verification, final SHA and
independent review.
